method_atom: A complete classification of the regular representations of a set of non-commuting operators obeying scale-covariant Lie-algebra relations of the form [A, B_j] = (i/k) B_j, followed by a canonical (Weyl) quantization of the associated commutative manifold into an operator algebra. The construction is intrinsically 'radial' — only a distinguished variable and a radial distance become non-commuting while angular variables stay classical — admits a symbolic calculus equipped with a trace functional on symbols, and features a spectral singularity along a distinguished axis that becomes topologically disconnected in the large-scale limit. It yields joint uncertainty relations among the conjugate quantities that can be made simultaneously arbitrarily small near the origin but grow with distance from it.
object_atom: k-Minkowski quantum spacetime: the time coordinate T and spatial coordinates X_j of R x R^d treated as non-commuting operators at the Planck scale, with the prediction that such quantum-spacetime uncertainty effects could become visible at LHC energies for processes localised within roughly 1mm of the spacetime origin.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the representation-theoretic classification, Weyl quantization, symbolic/trace calculus, and uncertainty relations for scale-covariant non-commuting variables; the domain-specific part is that these variables are the time and space coordinates of Planck-scale spacetime probed at collider energies.
